title: Sequence-consistent HSMM/HMM post-processor over driver-race trajectories
summary: Pit decisions are sequential and duration-constrained: tyre age evolves smoothly, then resets after a stop.
rationale: Pit decisions are sequential and duration-constrained: tyre age evolves smoothly, then resets after a stop. Hidden semi-Markov models are designed for latent states with explicit durations, which matches stint progression better than independent row scoring.
implementation_hint: Keep the current classifier as an emission model, then run a lightweight per-driver, per-race decoder with latent states like `normal stint`, `pit-imminent`, `pit-lap aftermath`. Use duration penalties so `pit-imminent` cannot persist unrealistically and enforce reset consistency around observed `PitStop`. Compare raw scores vs decoded probabilities.
expected_effect: Should reduce spurious isolated spikes and sharpen probability mass around true stop windows, potentially helping AUC by improving ordering within each sequence.
risk: If transition assumptions are too rigid, the decoder can oversmooth and hurt leaderboard generalization; needs strict validation by race-year groups.
